Press enter or click to view image in full size

For years, whenever we wanted to focus an input after resetting a form or displaying validation errors, the solution usually looked something like this:

• viewChild()

• ElementRef

• nativeElement.focus()
It worked
But it also meant directly interacting with the DOM, keeping references to elements, and writing code that Angular couldn’t really understand.
With Angular 21.1 Signal Forms, there’s now a much cleaner solution:
focusBoundControl()
Instead of telling the browser which DOM element to focus, you simply tell the form to focus the control that is bound to your model.
Even better
It also automatically scrolls the element into view when necessary.
The Traditional Approach
Most Angular developers have written code similar to this.
import { Component, ElementRef, viewChild } from '@angular/core';@Component({
selector: 'app-profile',
templateUrl: './profile.html',
})
export class ProfileComponent {
readonly emailInput =
viewChild<ElementRef<HTMLInputElement>>('emailInput')…
